reported_entities.name: Apple
reported_entities.claim: TSMC's largest named customer; Morgan Stanley projects Apple at ~16-25% of TSMC 2026 revenue mix; long-term capacity reservations at leading nodes.

reported_entities.name: Nvidia
reported_entities.claim: Projected single biggest named customer at ~20% of TSMC 2026 revenue (Morgan Stanley); one of the first N2 (2nm) customers.

reported_entities.name: Broadcom
reported_entities.claim: ~11-15% of TSMC 2026 revenue mix; rapidly emerging, could overtake Nvidia as TSMC's second-largest client; pursuing custom AI ASICs fabbed at TSMC.

reported_entities.name: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD)
reported_entities.claim: ~7-8% of TSMC 2026 revenue mix; among first N2 (2nm) customers; AMD's Venice processor (2026 launch) built on TSMC 2nm.

reported_entities.name: MediaTek
reported_entities.claim: ~9-10% of TSMC 2026 revenue mix; among first N2 (2nm) node customers.

reported_entities.name: Qualcomm
reported_entities.claim: ~8% of TSMC 2026 revenue mix; among initial N2 (2nm) customers alongside Nvidia, Apple, AMD.

reported_entities.name: Intel
reported_entities.claim: ~7% of TSMC 2026 revenue mix per analyst estimates; despite being a foundry rival, Intel outsources some products to TSMC.

reported_entities.name: Marvell Technology
reported_entities.claim: Introduced first 2nm silicon IP for next-gen AI/cloud infrastructure on TSMC's N2 node; part of TSMC's ~15 confirmed N2 clients.

reported_entities.name: Amazon (AWS)
reported_entities.claim: AWS custom AI/ASIC chips (e.g., Trainium/Graviton family) fabbed at TSMC; expected to take significant share of TSMC N2 production for next-gen ASIC architectures.

reported_entities.name: Google
reported_entities.claim: Google's TPU line (e.g., 6th-gen Trillium) manufactured on TSMC processes; pursuing custom AI chips on TSMC's advanced nodes including N2.

reported_entities.name: Microsoft
reported_entities.claim: Listed among hyperscalers (alongside Amazon, Google) as initial TSMC 2nm (N2) customers for custom silicon.

notes: Salva discovery run completed with retrieval_health=ok but qualified_count=0 (no entities passed qualification filters for this objective/intent combo), so no Salva-sourced entities were available to report. Supplemented entirely via 2 WebSearch queries covering TSMC's disclosed revenue-mix customer breakdown (Apple/Nvidia/Broadcom/MediaTek/Qualcomm/AMD/Intel) and its N2 (2nm) node customer list (adds Marvell, Amazon/AWS, Google, Microsoft as hyperscaler/ASIC customers). All entries are well-documented via analyst estimates (Morgan Stanley via SEC filings context), TrendForce, and Tom's Hardware reporting.
